name: Simple direct feedback — tests surgical modification vs rebuild

input.userMessage: /revise make the sidebar narrower and change the page title to 'Analytics Overview'

expectedBehaviors: Skips the feedback source question — user already provided feedback inline
Classifies both items: sidebar width = principle-based, title = content-based
Both are directly actionable — no questions needed
Changes ONLY the sidebar width and title text — nothing else
Uses design tokens for the new sidebar width (not a random pixel value)
Takes one before screenshot and one after screenshot, not per-change
antiPatterns: Asks 'where is the feedback coming from?' when it was already provided inline
Redesigns the entire sidebar layout instead of just narrowing it
Changes other elements while 'fixing' the sidebar
Hardcodes a pixel width instead of using a token
Takes a screenshot after each of the 2 changes (should batch)

evaluationCriteria.skipUnnecessaryQuestions: Does it go straight to classification without asking for feedback source?
evaluationCriteria.surgicalPrecision: Does it change ONLY what was asked — sidebar width and title text?
evaluationCriteria.nonDestructive: Does it clone the frame before modifying?
evaluationCriteria.screenshotEconomy: Does it take 1 batch screenshot, not 2 per-change screenshots?
